100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On April 30, 1926, the British Parliament faced mounting pressure as the General Council of the Trades Union Congress demanded immediate action to address rising unemployment. Meanwhile, in the United States, the Supreme Court upheld the constitutionality of the National Industrial Recovery Act, a pivotal move in the New Deal era. In international news, Egypt announced plans for a significant military buildup, raising tensions in the region.
Updated: Thursday, April 30, 2026 at 08:11:32 AM CDT
